chief_complaint: A 3-month-old infant presented with fever, irritability, and lethargy.
history: The infant had been experiencing recurrent viral infections and had a history of lymphocytopenia. Immunophenotyping revealed low absolute counts of T, B, and natural killer cells. The infant had not been receiving any medications and had no known allergies.
exam: Vital signs: temperature 38.5°C, heart rate 160 bpm, respiratory rate 40 breaths/min. Physical examination revealed a lethargic infant with a bulging fontanelle.
labs: Complete blood count: lymphocyte count 0.2 x 10^9/L, platelet count 50 x 10^9/L. Immunophenotyping: low absolute counts of T, B, and natural killer cells. Cerebrospinal fluid analysis: human parvovirus B-19 DNA detected.
correct_diagnosis: Primary immunodeficiency with human parvovirus B-19 meningitis
differentials: Primary immunodeficiency with human parvovirus B-19 meningitis
Meningitis due to other viral pathogens
correct_ranking: Primary immunodeficiency with human parvovirus B-19 meningitis
Meningitis due to other viral pathogens
